pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8) and DOT/UN chemical database

CAMEO Chemicals protective clothing section: specifies 'appropriate personal protective clothing' and 'SCBA should be used whenever possible' indicating standard gear provides basic protection adequate for response
CAMEO Chemicals Acetone page explicitly states 'Whenever possible, Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus (SCBA) should be used'
CAMEO Chemicals: standard gear provides limited chemical protection, not inadequate; classified as flammable liquid with manageable hazards
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2862) and DOT/UN database

CAMEO Chemicals explicitly requires 'positive pressure breathing apparatus and FULL protective clothing'; DuPont Tychem fabrics show immediate breakthrough times indicating standard structural gear is inadequate; ERG Guide 118 emphasizes specialized protective requirements
CAMEO Chemicals Chlorine page: 'Wear positive pressure breathing apparatus and full protective clothing'
CAMEO Chemicals: toxic gas requiring full protective clothing and positive pressure breathing, indicating structural gear will NOT provide adequate protection
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/14746) and DOT/UN database

CAMEO Chemicals explicitly states 'Only special protective clothing should be worn' and 'A few whiffs of gas, or liquid penetrating firefighter's protective clothing, could be fatal'; requires 'positive pressure breathing apparatus and special protective clothing'; ERG Guide 117 indicates standard gear is insufficient
CAMEO Chemicals Hydrogen Cyanide page: 'Wear positive pressure breathing apparatus and special protective clothing'
CAMEO Chemicals: deadly blood agent requiring special protective clothing beyond structural gear; standard gear explicitly inadequate
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654) and DOT/UN database

CAMEO Chemicals protective clothing: specifies 'appropriate personal protective clothing' and 'SCBA should be used whenever possible' for unknown atmospheres; classified as flammable liquid with standard response procedures
CAMEO Chemicals Toluene page: 'Whenever possible, Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus (SCBA) should be used'
CAMEO Chemicals: standard gear provides limited chemical protection; classified as flammable liquid with manageable hazards for response
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4228) and DOT/UN database

CAMEO Chemicals emphasizes 'Air-purifying or supplied-air respiratory equipment should also be worn, as necessary' and describes extreme toxicity (NFPA Health 4 - lethal); warning notes that standard structural gear alone provides inadequate protection for this choking agent
CAMEO Chemicals Phosgene page: explicitly requires supplied-air or air-purifying respiratory equipment
CAMEO Chemicals: choking agent with extreme toxicity requiring supplied-air equipment; standard structural gear will NOT provide adequate protection
Task configuration specifies this as the output location

privileged_info_summary: All metadata ground truth values are verified as accurate. Data comes from the real CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), a legitimate NOAA-maintained hazmat database. All five UN numbers are correct (Acetone 1090, Chlorine 1017, Hydrogen Cyanide 1051, Toluene 1294, Phosgene 1076). SCBA is required for all five chemicals. Gear adequacy classifications are verified: Acetone and Toluene (LEVEL_B, standard gear provides limited protection) require only standard protective clothing plus SCBA; Chlorine, Hydrogen Cyanide, and Phosgene (LEVEL_A, standard gear inadequate) require special/full protective clothing plus positive pressure breathing apparatus. The protective clothing recommendations are found on each chemical's CAMEO page under the 'Protective Clothing' section.
